str:translations.str:about_feeds: This is a web feed, also known as an Atom feed. Subscribe by copying the URL from the address bar into your newsreader. Visit About Feeds to learn more and get started. It's free.

feed.title: ~/nbaig-dev - tips-for-success
feed.subtitle: I am a backend web developer with 14+ years of experience. In this blog I will be posting my journey with the Rust Programming Language.

entry.title: Overcoming the Challenges of Being a Non-Traditional Student in Tech
entry.summary: In the ever-evolving world of technology, breaking into the tech industry as a non-traditional student can pose unique challenges. Older students, working professionals, and those with other obligations may find it challenging to balance their studies, work, and personal life while navigating the dynamic landscape of tech. However, these challenges are not insurmountable, and in this blog post, we explore ten practical tips for overcoming them. From identifying your learning style to seeking out mentors, building a portfolio, staying up-to-date with industry trends, collaborating with others, and being persistent, our tips aim to help non-traditional students achieve success in tech. Whether you are just starting your journey into the tech world or looking for ways to improve your chances of breaking through, this post is here to support and guide you. So, if you are a non-traditional student looking to break into tech, read on, embrace the challenge, and let's work together towards success!

entry.title: How to Deal with the Pressure of Being a Tech Mentor
entry.summary: In this blog post, we explore the challenges and pressure that come with being a tech mentor and provide tips for success. We cover topics such as defining your role, establishing boundaries, effective communication, building a support network, staying up-to-date, encouraging independence, providing feedback, managing time effectively, active listening, and celebrating successes. Our aim is to help tech mentors navigate the unique demands of their role and build fulfilling and successful mentoring relationships. By implementing these strategies, we hope that you can thrive as a tech mentor and make a meaningful impact on your mentees' learning journey. Share this post with others in the tech community to promote a more supportive and connected ecosystem!

entry.title: Balancing Tech Work and Parenthood: Tips for Success
entry.summary: In today's fast-paced tech industry, where long work hours and tight deadlines are the norm, striking a healthy balance between work and parenthood can be a daunting task for many parents. However, with the right strategies and support, it is possible to find harmony between these two important facets of life. In this blog post, we've compiled 10 practical tips that can help you navigate the complex dance of balancing tech work and parenthood. Whether you're a working mom or dad, a seasoned professional or new to the industry, these tips will provide actionable insights that you can use to find a sustainable path forward. From establishing clear boundaries and finding work that aligns with your values to embracing technology and seeking out community support, we believe that by following these suggestions, you'll be well on your way to finding a healthy, sustainable balance between tech work and parenthood. So, whether you're just starting out or have been navigating this complex dance for years, we invite you to read on and share your own experiences and tips in the comments below. Together, we can build a supportive community of working parents who can learn from and support one another as we navigate this exciting but often challenging journey!
